channel.title: IBD LitMonitor - Quality of Life & PROs
channel.description: IBD literature monitoring: Quality of Life & PROs

item.title: Engineering immune cell therapies for inflammatory bowel disease: from stem cells to CAR T cells.
item.description: Nature reviews. Gastroenterology & hepatology - Review - Despite the development of novel small molecules and antibody-based therapies targeting pro-inflammatory immune pathways, the clinical management of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) remains challen...

item.title: Cajaninstilbene Acid in Macrophage Immunometabolism and Angiogenesis: Mechanisms and Therapeutic Potential.
item.description: Pharmacological research - Review - Macrophage immunometabolism is an important regulator of inflammatory resolution and angiogenic responses. Aberrant macrophage polarization, accompanied by metabolic disturbances such as enhanced g...

item.title: Association Between Web-Based Patient Portal Messaging and Disease Activity in Inflammatory Bowel Disease: Retrospective Pilot Cohort Study.
item.description: JMIR human factors - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) requires ongoing monitoring and effective communication between patients and health care providers. Web-based patient portals may enhance engagement, but their asso...

item.title: Reversal of Frailty and Improvement in Quality of Life Following Advanced Therapy Initiation in Patients with Inflammatory Bowel Disease: A Prospective Cohort Study.
item.description: Medicina (Kaunas, Lithuania) - Observational study - Background and Objectives: In recent years, frailty has emerged as a prognostic factor in inflammatory bowel diseases (IBD), particularly among patients with active disease. However, evidence regar...

item.title: Honeysuckle-derived nanovesicles alleviate inflammatory bowel disease-associated neuropsychiatric disorders via the gut-brain axis.
item.description: Theranostics - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is frequently accompanied by anxiety and depression, yet effective therapies targeting both intestinal inflammation and neuropsychiatric symptoms remain limited. Th...
